name: Housing Assistance And Development Services, Inc.
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit credit counseling and housing assistance agency in Kentucky offering financial education, foreclosure prevention, and affordable housing for student parents.
description_long:
HANDS Inc. is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization established in May 1993, headquartered in Bowling Green, Kentucky with service areas extending to Owensboro. The organization operates as a HUD-approved credit counseling agency, positioning it within the legitimate non-profit housing and financial counseling sector rather than for-profit lending or credit repair.

HANDS offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost financial counseling services including pre-purchase homebuyer counseling, budget counseling, credit counseling, and loss mitigation/foreclosure prevention assistance. They provide financial literacy classes covering mortgage loans and home selection. The organization operates Scholar House, an affordable apartment complex exclusively for full-time students with dependent children under 18, offering 2BR/1BA units with utilities included, on-site childcare, computer access, and community resources.

The organization distinguishes itself through its integrated approach combining housing assistance with mandatory financial education. Scholar House residents must attend 3 life skills classes annually (including parenting), participate in tenant meetings, and maintain academic performance (2.0+ GPA). This represents a supportive housing model rather than simple housing provision. HANDS explicitly targets low-income and student-parent populations often excluded from traditional housing and credit markets.

HANDS operates transparently with published contact information, physical addresses, and service offerings. As a HUD-approved counseling agency, it meets federal standards for housing counseling. The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are concentrated in central/western Kentucky (Bowling Green and Owensboro). Some services carry modest fees ($25-$42 for home ownership courses and credit reports), though core counseling is described as available. The organization represents legitimate non-profit assistance rather than quick-fix solutions.
pros: HUD-approved credit counseling agency meeting federal housing counseling standards
501(c)(3) non-profit structure with transparent operations and published staff/board information
Integrated Scholar House program providing affordable housing (utilities included) plus mandatory financial education for student parents
Comprehensive services including pre-purchase counseling, budget counseling, credit counseling, and foreclosure loss mitigation
On-site support services at Scholar House including Head Start program, childcare, 24/7 computer lab, and community spaces
Low-cost fee structure ($25-$42 for specific services) with emphasis on accessibility
Established organization (30+ years) with dual office locations and extended service area
cons: Geographic limitation to Kentucky (Bowling Green and Owensboro areas only)
Limited publicly available information about counselor credentials, success metrics, or average outcomes
Scholar House has strict eligibility requirements (full-time student status, dependent children, specific enrolled schools, 2.0+ GPA maintenance) limiting accessibility
Website lacks detail on average time to resolve credit issues or housing outcomes
No evidence of online counseling options; appears to require in-person visits based on physical address listing
best_for: Student parents in Kentucky seeking affordable housing combined with financial education and childcare support
Low-income Kentucky residents needing HUD-approved pre-purchase homebuyer counseling before mortgage applications
Homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage loss seeking mitigation counseling in central/western Kentucky
Individuals seeking non-profit, low-cost credit counseling from a legitimate HUD-approved agency
diagnosis: HANDS Inc. is best for low-income and student-parent populations in Kentucky seeking legitimate, HUD-approved non-profit counseling combined with affordable housing and financial education. The main caveat is geographic limitation to central/western Kentucky and strict eligibility requirements for housing assistance that exclude many consumers seeking help.
services: Loan mitigation and foreclosure prevention counseling
Financial literacy classes on mortgage loans and home selection
Scholar House affordable rental housing (2BR/1BA with utilities included)
On-site childcare and Head Start programs at Scholar House
24/7 computer lab access for Scholar House residents
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2530 Scottsville Rd Ste 106A, Bowling Green, KY 42104
